name: Poached Salmon (Syd)
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the white film from the skin side of the salmon fillets.
2. Cut each fillet into 4 pieces, about 8 ounces each.
3. Scatter chopped scallions in a large skillet that is wide enough to hold the fillets in a single layer.
4. Butter one side of a piece of aluminum foil large enough to cover the skillet.
5. Pour white wine into the skillet and heat to simmering.
6. Sprinkle salmon lightly with salt and pepper.
7. Gently slip the fillets into the simmering wine.
8. Add cold water if needed to partially submerge the fillets.
9. Place the foil, buttered side down, on top of the fish.
10. Simmer gently until the tops of the fillets are opaque and nearly firm, 8-10 minutes.
11. Remove from heat and let cool in the liquid for at least 10 minutes.
12. Salmon can be served warm or at room temperature.
13. To serve, carefully lift the fillets using a large slotted spatula and place them on dinner plates.
